_comment: WC2026 Group I, Matchday 2 (Lincoln Financial Field, Philadelphia). MD1: France 3-1 Senegal; Iraq 1-4 Norway (leaky). Odds France -700 (1.143) / Draw +750 (8.50) / Iraq +2500 (26.0). Calibrated in line with the market (~85%): heavy favorite vs a leaky side (NOT a proven low block), so France should score freely. Corner lambda kept modest per the Argentina lesson (dominance != corners).

meta.stakes: France (3-1 Senegal) can secure qualification; Iraq (1-4 Norway, leaky) under pressure. Mbappe is 2 goals off the all-time WC scoring record.

home.name: France
notes: Elite attack (Mbappe, Dembele-type pace); beat Senegal 3-1. attack 1.55.
Calibrated in line with the market (~85%): heavy favorite, don't go below.
Top defense (Saliba, Upamecano) -> very low Iraq lambda, defense 0.65.

away.name: Iraq
notes: Leaky: conceded 4 to Norway -> defense 1.40. NOT a proven low block, so France should score multiple (unlike the Cape Verde/Iran cerrojos).
Weak attack vs an elite backline; scored 1 vs Norway -> attack 0.55.
May sit deep but its defense is poor, so few clean-sheet hopes -> rho -0.06.

cards._referee: Drew Fischer (Canada), CONFIRMED. CONCACAF referee, career ~2.7-3.3 YC/match (low-to-moderate). International discount + small bump for a lopsided game (Iraq tactical fouls). Expect a low-card game.
